text: Spermatogenesis is a multiphase process of cellular transformation that produces mature male gametes known as sperm for sexual reproduction. The process of spermatogenesis can be broken down into 3 phases: the mitotic proliferation of spermatogonia, meiosis, and post meiotic differentiation (spermiogenesis) (Boulanger et al., 2015). Male fertility is dependent on the quantity as well as the proper cellular morphology of the sperm formed in the testes. The fusion of sperm and oocytes is the key step for the beginning of life known as fertilization. Oocyte fertilization and the production of viable offspring from sexual reproduction are dependent on spermatogenesis and sufficient quantity and quality of sperm. When the impairment of spermatogenesis occurs, it can result in impaired reproduction with a decrease in viable offspring.
